en: I ask, then, has God rejected his people? By no means! For I myself am an Israelite, a descendant of Abraham, a member of the tribe of Benjamin.
zh: 那麼我要說，難道 神丟棄了他的子民嗎？絕對沒有！因為我自己也是以色列人，是亞伯拉罕的後裔，屬於便雅憫支派的。

en: God has not rejected his people whom he foreknew. Do you not know what the Scripture says of Elijah, how he appeals to God against Israel?
zh: 神並沒有丟棄他預先知道的子民。難道你們不知道在經上以利亞的話是怎樣說的嗎？他向 神控訴以色列人：

en: "Lord, they have killed your prophets, they have demolished your altars, and I alone am left, and they seek my life."
zh: “主啊，他們殺了你的眾先知，拆毀了你的祭壇，只剩下我一個人，他們還在尋索我的性命。”

en: But what is God's reply to him? "I have kept for myself seven thousand men who have not bowed the knee to Baal."
zh: 但 神怎樣回答他呢？“我為自己留下了七千人，是沒有向巴力屈膝的。”

en: So too at the present time there is a remnant, chosen by grace.
zh: 因此，現在也是這樣，按著恩典的揀選，還有剩下的餘數。

en: But if it is by grace, it is no longer on the basis of works; otherwise grace would no longer be grace.
zh: 既然是靠著恩典，就不再是由於行為了；不然的話，恩典就不再是恩典了。

en: What then? Israel failed to obtain what it was seeking. The elect obtained it, but the rest were hardened,
zh: 那又怎麼樣呢？以色列人懇切尋找的，他們沒有得到，蒙揀選的人倒得著了。其餘的人都成了頑固的，

en: as it is written, "God gave them a spirit of stupor, eyes that would not see and ears that would not hear, down to this very day."
zh: 正如經上所記： “ 神給了他們麻木的靈， 有眼睛卻看不見， 有耳朵卻聽不到， 直到今日。”

en: And David says, "Let their table become a snare and a trap, a stumbling block and a retribution for them;
zh: 大衛也說： “願他們的筵席成為他們的網羅、陷阱、 絆腳石和報應；

en: let their eyes be darkened so that they cannot see, and bend their backs forever."
zh: 願他們的眼睛昏暗，不能看見； 願他們的背脊常常彎曲。”

en: So I ask, did they stumble in order that they might fall? By no means! Rather through their trespass salvation has come to the Gentiles, so as to make Israel jealous.
zh: 那麼我要說，他們失足是要倒下去嗎？絕對不是！反而因為他們的過犯，救恩就臨到外族人，為了要激起他們奮發。

en: Now if their trespass means riches for the world, and if their failure means riches for the Gentiles, how much more will their full inclusion mean!
zh: 既然他們的過犯可以使世人富足，他們的失敗可以使外族人富足，何況他們的豐盛呢？

en: Now I am speaking to you Gentiles. Inasmuch then as I am an apostle to the Gentiles, I magnify my ministry
zh: 我現在對你們外族人說話，因為我是外族人的使徒，所以尊重我的職分，

en: in order somehow to make my fellow Jews jealous, and thus save some of them.
zh: 這樣也許可以激起我骨肉之親奮發，使他們中間有一些人得救。

en: For if their rejection means the reconciliation of the world, what will their acceptance mean but life from the dead?
zh: 如果他們被捨棄，世人就可以與 神復和；他們蒙接納，不就等於從死人中復活嗎？

en: If the dough offered as firstfruits is holy, so is the whole lump, and if the root is holy, so are the branches.
zh: 如果首先獻上的生麵是聖的，整團麵也是聖的；如果樹根是聖的，樹枝也是聖的。

en: But if some of the branches were broken off, and you, although a wild olive shoot, were grafted in among the others and now share in the nourishing root of the olive tree,
zh: 如果把幾根樹枝折下來，讓你這野橄欖可以接上去，一同分享那橄欖樹根的汁漿，

en: do not be arrogant toward the branches. If you are, remember it is not you who support the root, but the root that supports you.
zh: 你就不可向那些樹枝誇口。你若要誇口，就應當想想：不是你支持著樹根，而是樹根支持著你。

en: Then you will say, "Branches were broken off so that I might be grafted in."
zh: 那麼你會說，那些樹枝被折下來，就是要把我接上去。

en: That is true. They were broken off because of their unbelief, but you stand fast through faith. So do not become proud, but stand in awe.
zh: 不錯，他們因為不信而被折下來，你因著信才站立得住。只是不可心高氣傲，倒要存畏懼的心。

en: For if God did not spare the natural branches, neither will he spare you.
zh: 神既然不顧惜那本來的樹枝，也不會顧惜你。

en: Note then the kindness and the severity of God: severity toward those who have fallen, but God's kindness to you, provided you continue in his kindness. Otherwise you too will be cut off.
zh: 所以要留意 神的恩慈和嚴厲：對跌倒的人，他是嚴厲的；對你，只要你繼續在他的恩慈裡，他是恩慈的；不然的話，你也會被砍下來。

en: And even they, if they do not continue in their unbelief, will be grafted in, for God has the power to graft them in again.
zh: 至於他們，如果不是繼續不信，他們仍然會被接上去，因為 神能夠把他們再接上去。

en: For if you were cut from what is by nature a wild olive tree, and grafted, contrary to nature, into a cultivated olive tree, how much more will these, the natural branches, be grafted back into their own olive tree.
zh: 你這從野生的橄欖樹上砍下來的，尚且可以不自然地接在栽種的橄欖樹上，那些本來就有的樹枝，不是更能夠接在原來的橄欖樹上嗎？

en: Lest you be wise in your own conceits, I want you to understand this mystery, brothers: a partial hardening has come upon Israel, until the fullness of the Gentiles has come in.
zh: 弟兄們，我不願意你們對這奧祕一無所知，免得你們自以為聰明。這奧祕就是以色列人當中有一部分是硬心的，直到外族人的全數滿了；

en: And in this way all Israel will be saved, as it is written, "The Deliverer will come from Zion, he will banish ungodliness from Jacob";
zh: 這樣，全以色列都要得救，如經上所記： “拯救者必從錫安出來， 除掉雅各家的不敬虔的心；

en: "and this will be my covenant with them when I take away their sins."
zh: 我除去他們罪惡的時候， 就與他們立這樣的約。”

en: As regards the gospel, they are enemies of God for your sake. But as regards election, they are beloved for the sake of their forefathers.
zh: 就福音來說，因你們的緣故，他們是仇敵；就揀選來說，因祖宗的緣故，他們是蒙愛的。

en: For the gifts and the calling of God are irrevocable.
zh: 因為 神的恩賞和呼召是決不會反悔的。

en: Just as you were at one time disobedient to God but now have received mercy because of their disobedience,
zh: 正如你們從前不順服 神，現在卻因著他們的不順服，你們倒蒙了憐憫；

en: so they too have now been disobedient in order that by the mercy shown to you they also may now receive mercy.
zh: 照樣，他們因著你們所蒙的憐憫，現在也不順服，使他們現在也可以蒙憐憫。

en: For God has consigned all to disobedience, that he may have mercy on all.
zh: 因為 神把所有的人都圈在不順服之中，為了要憐憫所有的人。

en: Oh, the depth of the riches and wisdom and knowledge of God! How unsearchable are his judgments and how inscrutable his ways!
zh: 神的豐富、智慧和知識，是多麼高深啊！他的判斷是多麼難測，他的道路是多麼難尋！

en: "For who has known the mind of the Lord, or who has been his counselor?"
zh: “誰知道主的心意， 誰作過他的參謀？”

en: "Or who has given a gift to him that he might be repaid?"
zh: “誰先給了他， 以致他要償還呢？”

en: For from him and through him and to him are all things. To him be glory forever. Amen.
zh: 因為萬有都是本於他，倚靠他，歸於他。願榮耀歸給他，直到永遠。阿們。
